TensorFlow tf.math.top_k only supports reduction along the last dimension (axis=-1), while MindSpore topk supports arbitrary dim (e.g., dim=1). The shape mismatch [2,3,4,5] vs [2,3,4,3] occurs because TF reduces on last dim (5→3), yielding output shape [2,3,4,3], but MS reduces on dim=1 (3→3), yielding [2,3,4,5]→[2,3,4,5] with k=3 kept in dim=1 → [2,3,4,5]→[2,3,4,5] is wrong; actual MS output shape should be [2,3,4,5] with dim=1 → size-3 dim, so shape becomes [2,3,4,5] → [2,3,4,5] with dim=1 reduced: [2,3,4,5] → [2,3,4,5] → no, correct: input (2,3,4,5), dim=1 → axis 1 of size 3, k=3 ⇒ output shape same as input but dim1 replaced by k ⇒ [2,3,4,5] → [2,3,4,5] is invalid; actually: dim=1 means reduce along axis 1, so output shape is [2,k,4,5] = [2,3,4,5]. But TF has no dim arg and always uses axis=-1 ⇒ output shape [2,3,4,3]. To align semantics, TF test case must emulate dim=1 reduction via transpose + top_k + transpose, but per task constraints we repair by making both operate on last dim: set dim=-1 in MS and keep TF as-is.
